name: Coach Marks / Spotlights / Tooltips

fields.definition_origin: 'Coach mark' as a term entered the design lexicon via Apple's iOS Springboard hints (post-iOS7 ~2013) and Google's Material Design feature discovery (2014-2016). The pattern has 1990s-era predecessors in desktop tooltips (Microsoft Office Clippy, 1996, infamously over-applied) and Adobe's contextual help. Material Design Feature Discovery spec (2018, deprecated and revised in M3) documents the canonical 'tap target highlight + descriptive sheet' interaction. NN/g classifies coach marks as 'contextual onboarding'. YouTube Android's contextual feature highlights (Library, Studio, Shorts entry) are the canonical implementation cited in PDF Deep Dive and DesignerUp. Industry tooling matured 2020-2024: Plotline, Appcues Mobile, Pendo Mobile, Product Fruits all ship coach-mark primitives. Distinct from interactive walkthroughs (item 23) — coach marks are READ-ONLY hints; walkthroughs require user action.
fields.core_mechanism: Operates via just-in-time learning (Carroll's minimalist instruction, 1990) — instruction delivered at the moment of need is retained better than instruction delivered abstractly upfront. Mechanism stack: (1) attention focus via spotlight/dim — the highlighted element wins visual hierarchy (Wickens attention theory); (2) contextual encoding (Tulving 1972 encoding specificity) — learning paired with context cues recalls better; (3) reduced extraneous cognitive load (Sweller 1988) — only the relevant feature's affordance is visible while learning it; (4) curiosity-resolution (Loewenstein 1994) — coach marks fire when user enters unfamiliar surface, resolving the 'what is this?' gap immediately; (5) optional dismissal preserves user autonomy (Self-Determination Theory, item 5) — power users can skip without penalty. Critical contrast: COLD-LAUNCH coach marks (firing on first app open) are ineffective because there's no context to anchor; CONTEXTUAL coach marks (firing when user navigates to unfamiliar surface) succeed because they answer a question the user is actually forming.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Spatial attention engages parietal cortex (Posner 1980 attention orienting). Contextual encoding-specificity engages hippocampus (Tulving). Curiosity-driven attention engages midbrain dopaminergic systems (Gruber et al. 2014 — curiosity facilitates learning). [uncertain on direct mobile-coach-mark fMRI]
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) YouTube Android's contextual feature highlights — canonical cited in PDF Deep Dive and DesignerUp '14 Types of Onboarding UX' as the model for triggering on unfamiliar-surface entry, NOT cold launch. (2) NN/g case studies on tooltips show contextual placement outperforms upfront introduction by 30-50% recall. (3) Material Design Feature Discovery spec (2018-2024) — formal usability testing referenced in Material guidance. (4) Pendo product analytics 2023 report: in-app tooltips fire-on-first-feature-discovery have ~40-60% engagement vs ~10-15% for cold-launch carousel. (5) Plotline blog (industry tooling, 2024) reports A/B-tested coach-mark conversion lifts of 15-30% on feature adoption when contextually placed. (6) Apple HIG (2024 update): explicitly warns against 'guided tours' on first launch and prefers 'just-in-time hints'. (7) Appcues Mobile case studies (2024) on 50+ apps show contextual coach-mark patterns averaging 3-5% feature adoption lift per surface vs no-coach-mark baseline. (8) Carroll's minimalist instruction (1990) is the foundational research — replicated extensively in instructional design. (9) Counter-evidence: Material Design's original Feature Discovery spec (2016 version) was widely over-used and led to coach-mark fatigue in Google's own apps; M3 (2024) softens to 'use sparingly'.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Feature adoption rate per surface (% of users who use the feature within 7 days of first surface entry), tooltip dismissal vs engagement ratio (>70% engagement = good targeting), repeat-feature-use rate (one-time use vs habituation), surface-bounce-rate (do users immediately leave a surface they were coach-marked on?).
fields.evidence_strength: industry_benchmark + practitioner_opinion (foundational learning theory peer-reviewed; specific app A/B figures are vendor-published)
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Pendo / Appcues / Plotline industry data: contextual coach marks lift feature adoption 15-30% per surface; cold-launch coach-mark carousels show negative or zero lift. unverified at peer-reviewed RCT level for specific apps.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) Trigger logic: NEVER on cold launch. Fire when user navigates to a surface they have NOT visited before in their session history. Persist 'visited surfaces' set in MMKV / UserDefaults / SharedPreferences. (2) Single coach mark per surface visit — never stack 2+ tooltips simultaneously (cognitive overload). (3) Component anatomy: dim background overlay (60-70% black on iOS, lower on M3 since M3 prefers card-elevation), spotlight cutout around target element with 4-8px padding, descriptive sheet below or above target with arrow pointer, dismiss affordance (Got it / X / tap-anywhere). (4) Maximum 5 coach marks across the app's lifetime — beyond this triggers fatigue. (5) Persistence: once dismissed, never re-show same coach mark unless user explicitly opens 'Show tips' menu. (6) Animation: spotlight reveal in 280-320ms cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1); avoid bounce (childish for utilities, breaks reduced-motion). (7) Server-driven coach-mark registry — JSON config with target_id + trigger_predicate + copy + variant_id allows zero-deploy tuning. (8) Telemetry: emit `coach_mark_shown { id, target_surface }`, `coach_mark_engaged { id, action }`, `coach_mark_dismissed { id, action }`. (9) Cross-device sync: dismissal state should sync via backend so user doesn't see same hint on second device (cross-device hydration item 33). (10) Alternative pattern for power-feature reveal: 'What's New' modal on app update — but use sparingly.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) YouTube Android (canonical PDF cite): contextual highlight on first navigation to Library / Studio / Shorts entry — never on cold launch, only fires on first surface visit. (2) Apple Notes (iPadOS) — first time user opens drawing tools, tooltip introduces palette and brush picker. (3) Apple Photos — first time user enters Memories tab, brief contextual hint. (4) iOS Springboard — long-press hints fire at first contextual moment, never cold-launch. (5) Slack — first-time use of threads triggers a contextual coach mark on the thread sidebar. (6) Notion — first time user creates a database, brief tooltip on view-switcher. (7) Figma — first selection of a frame fires hint on auto-layout panel. (8) Spotify — first session ending triggers 'You discovered N songs today' hint at the right moment. (9) Linear — keyboard shortcut hints surface contextually as user does the slow-path equivalent. (10) Things 3 — Magic Plus button hint surfaces only once, on first launch, with explicit dismissal. (11) Google Maps — Live View / AR navigation triggers contextual coach-mark only when entering AR mode. (12) Pudding Monsters (gaming) — animated hand overlay (this is closer to walkthrough, item 23, but starts with coach-mark spotlight).
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile: spotlight cutout + bottom-sheet description is the canonical mobile pattern; thumb-zone considerations mean description sits above the target if target is in the bottom 1/3. Web equivalent: hover-tooltips (mouse-cursor based — NOT applicable mobile), pulsing-dot affordances on first-visit. Web has Driver.js, Shepherd.js, Intro.js libraries. Mobile-specific: integrates with native overlay primitives (UIView dimming, Compose Box, RN Reanimated 3 layered overlays).
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+): translucent material is purpose-built for the dim-overlay-with-spotlight pattern; bolder typography for the description sheet aligns with Liquid Glass alert style. M3 Expressive (Android 16+): M3 has DEPRECATED its original 'Feature Discovery' (cold-launch tap-target-with-circular-spotlight) in favor of softer contextual hints; new pattern uses elevated cards on first-visit. Coach marks must respect both DLs' motion preferences — no bouncing.
fields.category_fit: Strongest fit: complex apps with many surfaces (productivity, creative tools, B2B), apps with novel interaction primitives (gaming gestures, AR/Camera apps), apps with hidden power features (Keyboard shortcuts in Linear, Notion). Acceptable fit: media apps for advanced features (Spotify's collaborative playlists). WEAKEST fit: utility apps with universal value (don't need explanation), social apps with universal patterns (taps and swipes are learned). Specific exclusion: 'Do not use coach marks as substitute for good empty state seeding (item 21) — if user needs a coach mark to understand what to do post-onboarding, the empty state failed.'
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. Building a coach-mark primitive is 2-3 days; instrumentation and trigger logic is 2-3 days; copy authoring is half a day per coach mark. Tools like Plotline reduce this to hours but cost recurring fees. For a solo dev, building a minimal home-grown primitive is feasible.
fields.tooling_options: Plotline (mobile-native, server-driven), Appcues Mobile (multi-platform, paid tier), Pendo Mobile, Product Fruits, UserGuiding, Whatfix, Helppier. iOS Coach Marks open-source library (Freelance), react-native-copilot, react-native-spotlight-tour, Compose-Coachmark. Custom build: 200-400 lines using native overlay primitives. Avoid: Driver.js / Intro.js (web-only).
fields.ai_dependency: none. Coach marks are static UI primitives. Optional: small classifier (or rule engine) to choose WHICH coach mark to fire based on user behavior (e.g., user has not used dark mode → fire the dark-mode-toggle hint after 3 sessions). State: none required; rules engine sufficient.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude Design to build the coach-mark primitive as a contextual-trigger system. Prompt fragment: 'Build a CoachMark component with: `target_view_ref`, `trigger_predicate: () => boolean`, `copy: string`, `id: string`, `dismiss_persistence: "once" | "per_session"`. Trigger logic: NEVER fire on cold launch. Fire when user navigates to a surface for the first time in their session-history (persisted to MMKV/UserDefaults). Maximum ONE coach mark visible at a time across the app. Component renders: 65% black dimmed background overlay, spotlight cutout (4px padding) around target, description sheet (Cabinet Grotesk 17px) with arrow pointer toward target, single "Got it" CTA. Persist dismissal in user store; sync via backend. Forbid: cold-launch coach marks, stacked simultaneous coach marks, more than 5 coach marks across app lifetime, animated character mascots. Wire telemetry: coach_mark_shown / engaged / dismissed.'
fields.stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup a coach mark on a productivity app screen at 390x844. Target: a small "Tag" icon in the top-right of a note. Layer 1: 65% black overlay covering full screen. Layer 2: circular spotlight cutout (radius 32px) centered on the Tag icon, with 6px transparent padding. Layer 3: description sheet 280px below the icon, white card with 16px radius, 16px padding, contains: title "Tag your notes" (17px Cabinet Grotesk Semibold), body "Group related thoughts so you can find them later." (15px Söhne Regular), single "Got it" CTA (full-width text-only button, 44px tall). Sheet has small arrow pointer on top-right pointing toward the icon. Forbid: animated character mascots, swirling-gradient backgrounds, indigo-purple overlay, multi-step "Next/Previous" navigation in the sheet. ONE concept per coach mark.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen typically generates a cold-launch carousel of 5-10 'feature tour' tooltips on first app open — the explicit antipattern Apple HIG and M3 warn against. Specific failures: (1) deck-of-cards swipeable carousel masquerading as 'coach marks' (this is a Wall-of-Text antipattern, item 51), (2) all coach marks fire simultaneously on first launch, (3) coach marks stacked 2-3 at a time, (4) 'Next/Skip' navigation through 8 tour steps in one sitting, (5) animated mascot characters delivering the hints, (6) cold-launch placement with no context anchor. Counter-prompt: 'CONSTRAINT: coach marks are CONTEXTUAL not tour-based. NEVER fire on cold launch. Fire only when user enters a new surface for the first time in their session history. Maximum ONE coach mark visible simultaneously. Maximum 5 coach marks across the entire app lifetime. Forbid: deck-of-cards carousel, swipe-through tour, Next/Previous navigation between coach marks, mascot characters, cold-launch firing. Implement persistent visited-surface tracking in MMKV.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Cold-launch carousel of 5-10 coach marks (the canonical Wall-of-Text antipattern, Six-Antipattern #1 — and the explicit AI-codegen default). (2) Stacked simultaneous coach marks. (3) Coach mark fatigue (>5 across app lifetime). (4) Mandatory tour with no skip. (5) Mascot characters / Clippy-style animated agents (modern revival risk: AI assistants doing this badly in 2024-2026). (6) Coach marks compensating for poor IA (if 12 coach marks are needed to use the app, fix the IA instead). (7) Re-firing dismissed coach marks. (8) Coach marks on cold-start in lieu of empty state seeding (item 21). (9) Coach marks that block interaction (no escape until dismissed). (10) Generic copy ('Tap here to use this feature' — describe the OUTCOME not the action).
fields.regulatory_risk: Low. EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026 draft) flags 'manipulative consent UIs' but contextual coach marks for feature explanation are benign. Edge case: if coach marks pressure users into purchases or permissions (e.g., 'Tap here to upgrade' coach-marking the upgrade button), DFA could flag as dark pattern. Apple HIG: coach marks for paid feature should not block free-tier use. Accessibility regulations require coach marks to be screen-reader compatible.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple HIG 2024 explicitly warns against 'guided tours' on first launch — coach marks are acceptable only as contextual hints, not as cold-launch tutorials. Material 3 deprecated original Feature Discovery in favor of subtler contextual hints. Both: coach marks must dismiss easily (Apple Guideline 4.1 minimum functionality compliance). EU EAA 2025 (European Accessibility Act): coach marks must be screen-reader accessible.
fields.accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2: 1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus — tooltip-style coach marks must be dismissible, hoverable, persistent. Screen-reader: spotlight overlay must announce coach mark presence; VoiceOver focus must move to coach-mark sheet on appearance, return to original element on dismissal. Dynamic Type 200%: description sheet must reflow without truncation. prefers-reduced-motion: disable spotlight reveal animation, use crossfade. Avoid color-only spotlight (low-vision users) — pair with high-contrast outline. WCAG 2.2 2.5.8 (target size 24x24 css min): dismiss button must meet target size.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Coach-mark fatigue is real — every coach mark must earn its slot. Audit ruthlessly. (2) Trigger predicate complexity — many coach marks have fragile trigger conditions that fire wrongly (on app reinstall, on user switching, on session-history bug). Test extensively. (3) Localized copy must fit the description sheet at 200% Dynamic Type — 17px x 200% = 34px and a 4-line description on iPhone SE truncates badly. (4) A/B tests of coach-mark presence often show no aggregate lift even when individual surfaces benefit — measure per-surface, not app-wide. (5) Coach marks are a SYMPTOM of unintuitive UI — track which coach marks are most engaged and fix the underlying IA. (6) Animation budget on low-end Android devices — spotlight overlay can drop frames; provide reduced-motion fallback always.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Generally universal but copy register matters. JP/KR: more polite indirect copy ('You may find it useful to...' over 'Tap here to...'). LATAM: warmer/playful copy register accepted. MENA RTL: arrow pointer direction flips; layout mirrors. CN: super-app users tolerate denser hint UIs. Animated character hints (mascots) are more accepted in CN/JP/KR than US/EU markets, but still risky for international apps.
fields.prescribed_action: Use coach marks ONLY contextually — fire when user enters a new surface for the first time in their session history. Maximum 5 across app lifetime. NEVER cold-launch carousel. Single coach mark visible at a time. Persist dismissal in MMKV/UserDefaults; sync to backend for cross-device. If you need >5 coach marks to teach the app, the IA is broken — fix that instead.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: N/A — coach marks fire AFTER onboarding, contextually within the product. Questionnaire is upstream.
fields.aha_relevance: Indirect — coach marks help SECONDARY feature discovery after the core Aha is achieved. They are NOT the Aha mechanism. Treating coach marks as Aha tools (via cold-launch tour) is the canonical antipattern.
fields.measurement_horizon: Day_7 to Day_30 (feature adoption is a slow-burn metric; coach mark impact tested over time)
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Sage (clear, instructive, not pushy), Caregiver (helpful, tutor-like), Hero (just-in-time tip, no condescension). Mismatches: Outlaw (hint-style coach marks read as paternalistic; this archetype prefers raw exploration), Jester (coach marks should be playful, not earnest — risk of tonal clash), Magician (coach marks may break the illusion of magic — magic apps prefer empty seed-state over explicit hints).
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against 'No-Escape Room' (Six-Antipattern #3) when coach marks are mandatory and unskippable, and against 'Wall-of-Text + Cognitive Overload' (#1) when coach marks deck-of-cards stack on cold launch. Pairs against 'Ignored Error States' (#6) only adjacently — coach marks are NOT error remediation.
sources: [Carroll, John M. — The Nurnberg Funnel: Designing Minimalist Instruction (1990)](https://mitpress.mit.edu/9780262031639/the-nurnberg-funnel/)

uncertain: specific RCT-grade conversion-lift figures (vendor data is internal benchmarks, not peer-reviewed)
neuroscience_correlate (foundational attention research exists but no mobile-coach-mark fMRI)
